nodes.title: Validate inputs before iterating

anchor.snippet: if (!Array.isArray(arr)) throw new TypeError('arr must be an array');

nodes.why: Failing early gives clear errors and avoids confusing behavior later in the loop.
nodes.alternative: Skip checks and let runtime errors happen naturally.
nodes.tradeoff: Safer API with better error messages, at the cost of two quick guard checks.

nodes.title: Snapshot length and skip missing slots
nodes.why: Length snapshot avoids visiting newly pushed items, and `hasOwn` skips holes or deleted entries.
nodes.alternative: Loop with `i < arr.length` and call the callback on every index.
nodes.tradeoff: Closer to native `forEach` behavior, but the loop logic is a bit more detailed.

nodes.title: Use .call to preserve callback context
nodes.why: This keeps `thisArg` behavior consistent with native `forEach` and passes the expected callback arguments.
nodes.alternative: Invoke as `callbackFn(arr[i], i, arr)` without binding context.
nodes.tradeoff: More compatible semantics, with a tiny per-iteration function-call overhead.
